violate.example: If you violate the rules, you will leave.

vote.definition: To express a choice in an election.
vote.example: Citizens have the right to vote in elections.

weave.definition: To make fabric by crossing threads together.
weave.example: They use old machines to weave beautiful carpets.

whip.definition: To beat cream or eggs until thick.
whip.example: You need to whip the cream until it is thick.

whistle.definition: To make a high sound by blowing air.
whistle.example: She likes to whistle a tune while she works.

withdraw.definition: To take money out of an account.
withdraw.example: I need to withdraw some cash from the bank.

withstand.definition: To be strong enough to resist damage.
withstand.example: The strong walls can withstand any powerful storm.

witness.example: Did you witness the accident on the street?

yawn.definition: To open your mouth wide when tired.
yawn.example: The boring movie made everyone yawn repeatedly.

yearn.definition: To have an intense feeling of longing for something
yearn.example: Sometimes you just yearn for a quiet place to rest.

yield.definition: To produce or provide a natural product or result
yield.example: These investments will yield a high profit next year.
